name: HMS H 34 (N 34)
events: The history of HMS H 34 as compiled on this page is extracted from the patrol reports and logbooks of this submarine as well as several other files.
This page was last updated in December 2017.
12 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Rosyth. (1)
14 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) departed Rosyth for Harwich. She joined convoy FS 5 for the passage. (1)

16 Sep 1939 In the morning HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) arrived at Harwich. During the afternoon she conducted exercises off Harwich with HMS Drummer. During these exercises H 34 was attacked, in error, by a British aicraft of RAF 206 squadron. (1)
18 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) departed Harwich for Rosyth. (1)
20 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) arrived at Rosyth. (1)
23 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Rosyth. (1)
25 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) is docked in the floating dock (AFD 3) at the Rosyth Dockyard. (1)
27 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) is undocked. (1)
28 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) shifted from Rosyth to Aberdeen. (1)
29 Sep 1939 For the daily and attack positions of HMS H 34 during her 1st war patrol see the map below.

29 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) departed Aberdeen for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the outer Hebrides together with special service trawler HMS Euryalus (later to be renamed HMS Hoverfly) (Lt.Cdr. E.C.L. Day, RN). (3)

30 Sep 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. B.T. Simons, RN) fires two torpedoes at what is thought to be a German U-boat about 15 nautical miles north of Kinnaird Head in position 57°55'N, 02°03'W. Both torpedoes missed. As there was no German U-boat in the area the attack appears to be bogus.
0303 hours - The Officer of the Watch sighted a Uboat. Two torpedo tubes were then flooded and the Commanding Officer went to the bridge.
0315 hours - Fired two torpedoes. No hits were obtained.
0324 hours - A torpedo explosion was heard. Most likely this was one of the torpedoes hitting the bottom.
Later the same day HMS H 34 arrived at Scapa Flow. (3)

2 Oct 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) departed Scapa Flow to resume her 1st war patrol with the special service trawler HMS Euryalus (later to be renamed HMS Hoverfly) (Lt.Cdr. E.C.L. Day, RN). (3)
6 Oct 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) ended her 1st war patrol at 'Port A' (Loch Ewe). (3)
10 Oct 1939 For the daily positions of HMS H 34 during her 2nd war patrol see the map below.

10 Oct 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) departed 'Port A' (Loch Ewe) for her 2nd war patrol with the special service trawler HMS Euryalus (later to be renamed HMS Hoverfly) (Lt.Cdr. E.C.L. Day, RN). (3)
13 Oct 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) ended her 2nd war patrol at 'Port A' (Loch Ewe). (3)
17 Oct 1939 For the daily positions of HMS H 34 during her 3rd war patrol see the map below.

17 Oct 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) departed 'Port A' (Loch Ewe) for her 3rd war patrol with the special service trawler HMS Euryalus (later to be renamed HMS Hoverfly) (Lt.Cdr. E.C.L. Day, RN). (3)
22 Oct 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) ended her 3rd war patrol at 'Port A' (Loch Ewe). (3)

25 Oct 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) departed 'Port A' (Loch Ewe) for Rosyth. She was escorted by the special service trawler HMS Euryalus (later to be renamed HMS Hoverfly) (Lt.Cdr. E.C.L. Day, RN). (4)
26 Oct 1939 Around 1400 hours, HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN), was machine gunned twice by by a British fighter in position 58°36'N, 02°52'W (4)
27 Oct 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) arrived at Rosyth. (4)

11 Nov 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) departed Rothesay for Scapa Flow. She was escorted by HMS Maori (Cdr. G.N. Brewer, RN). H 34 was ordered to return to Rosyth shortly after departure. (5)

14 Nov 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) departed Rosyth for Scapa Flow together with the RFA (Royal Fleet Auxiliary) tanker War Pindari (5548 GRT, built 1920) (Master N. Colbridge). They were escorted by HMS Mashona (Cdr. P.V. McLaughlin, RN). (5)
15 Nov 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt.Cdr. B.T. Simons, RN) arrived at Scapa Flow. (5)
19 Nov 1939 Lt. Keith-Roach, RN, the first lieutenant took over command of HMS H 34 temporary as Lt.Cdr. Simons was taken to hospital with a head injury. (5)
20 Nov 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. M. Keith-Roach, RN) participated in A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with A/S trawlers. (5)
21 Nov 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. M. Keith-Roach, RN) participated in A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with A/S trawlers. (5)
25 Nov 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. M. Keith-Roach, RN) conducted exercises at Scapa Flow. (5)
29 Nov 1939 Lt. E.F. Balston took over command of HMS H 34 from her temporary commander Lt. Keith-Roach which resumed to his former role as first lieutenant. (5)
6 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) conducted independent exercises at Scapa Flow. (6)
10 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Isis (Cdr. J.C. Clouston, RN). (6)
13 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with local defence forces. (6)
14 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with local defence forces. (6)
19 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) departed Scapa Flow for special trials in the Pentland Firth with HMS Encounter (Lt.Cdr. E.V.St J. Morgan, RN). (6)

21 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) and HMS Encounter (Lt.Cdr. E.V.St J. Morgan, RN) returned to Scapa Flow. They departed again to continue the trials later the same day. When the trials were completed they proceeded to Dundee. (6)
23 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) arrived at Dundee. (6)
27 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) conducted special trials off Dundee. (6)
28 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) conducted special trials off Dundee. (6)
29 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) conducted special trials off Dundee. (6)
30 Dec 1939 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) conducted special trials off Dundee. (6)
4 Jan 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) shifted from Dundee to Rosyth. (7)
24 Jan 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN), ORP Wilk (Kpt.mar. (Lt.Cdr.) B.D. Krawczyk) and two destroyers conducted A/S exercises in the Firth of Forth. (8)

25 Jan 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Rosyth with HMS Vega (Cdr. C.I Horton, RN) and HMS Grimsby (Cdr. K.J. D'Arcy, RN). Upon completion of these exercises HMS H 34 departed Rosyth together with HMS Ursula (Cdr. G.C. Phillips, DSO, RN) and ORP Wilk (Kpt.mar. (Lt.Cdr.) B.D. Krawczyk). HMS Ursula was to proceed to Blyth, the other two submarines to Portsmouth. They were however ordered to return to Rosyth where they arrived the next day. The reason for them being recalled was due to the threat of enemy submarines operating along the east coast of the U.K. further to the south. (7)
1 Feb 1940 Around 1430 Z/1, HMS Ursula (Cdr. G.C. Phillips, DSO, RN) departed Rosyth for Blyth where she arrived around 0930 Z/2.
Around 1600 Z/1, HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) joined. This submarine too, was to proceed to Blyth.
Also joining at the same time was the destroyer HMS Encounter (Lt.Cdr. E.V.St J. Morgan, RN) which escorted the submarine until around 0335 Z/2, when she parted company to return to Rosyth. (9)

2 Feb 1940 HMS H 34 arrived at Blyth. HMS H 34 departed Blyth for Portsmouth later the same day together with HMS Unity (Lt. J.F.B. Brown, RN). They joined convoy FS 85 for passage towards the Nore. (10)
4 Feb 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) anchored off the Nore. (10)
7 Feb 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) and HMS Unity (Lt. J.F.B. Brown, RN) depated the Nore for Portsmouth. They were escorted by HMS Puffin (Lt.Cdr. Hon. J.M.G. Waldegrave, RN). (10)
8 Feb 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) arrived at Portsmouth. (10)
12 Feb 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) is docked at Portsmouth. (10)
27 Feb 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. E.F. Balston, RN) is undocked.

[No logs are available for HMS H 34 for the period of March - May 1940. No details of her operations are therefore known to us. H 34 was at Portsmouth the entire time being assigned to special trials and the training of new submarine crews.] (10)
29 May 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) departed Portland for her 4th war patrol. She is ordered to patrol in the English Channel.
[No log is available for this period so no map for this patrol can be displayed.] (3)
2 Jun 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) ended her 4th war patrol at Portsmouth. (3)

3 Jun 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN), HMS H 49 (Lt. M.A. Langley, RN) and HMS H 50 (Lt. A.R. Cheyne, RN) departed Portsmouth for Harwich. They were escorted by HMS Warrior II (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (11)
5 Jun 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) arrived at Harwich. (11)
8 Jun 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) departed Harwich for her 5th war patrol. She is ordered to patrol in the North Sea.
For the daily positions of HMS H 34 during this patrol see the map below.
15 Jun 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) ended her 5th war patrol at Harwich. (3)
23 Jun 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) departed Harwich for her 6th war patrol. She is ordered to patrol in the North Sea.
1 Jul 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) ended her 6th war patrol at Harwich. (3)
15 Jul 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) departed Harwich for her 7th war patrol. She is ordered to patrol in the North Sea.
23 Jul 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) ended her 7th war patrol at Lowestoft where she was immediately docked. (12)
31 Jul 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) was undocked. (12)
1 Aug 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) shifted from Lowestoft to Harwich. She was escorted by HMS Ben Bhrackie (T/Skr. H.J. May, RNR). (13)
5 Aug 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) departed Harwich for her 8th war patrol. She is ordered to patrol in the North Sea.
11 Aug 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) ended her 8th war patrol at Harwich. (3)
17 Aug 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) departed Harwich for her 9th war patrol. She is ordered to patrol in the North Sea.
26 Aug 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) ended her 9th war patrol at Harwich. (3)

1 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) departed Harwich for her 10th war patrol. This was an anti-invasion patrol off the East coast. H 34 was recalled later the same day. She then returned to Harwich. (14)
3 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) departed Harwich for Rosyth. Passage was made in convoy FN 71. (14)
5 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) arrived at Rosyth. (14)
6 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) departed Rosyth for Rothesay. H 34 joined convoy OA 210 for the passage. (14)
9 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (14)

10 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). . Upon completion of these exercises HMS H 34 proceeded to Campbeltown. (14)
16 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Terje 1 (Skr. C. Coultas, DSC, RNR) and HMS Terje 2 (Ch.Skr. G.R. Gale, RNR). (14)
17 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. A.F. Collett, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS St. Modwen (Cdr.(Retd.) W.A. Ford, RN) and HMS Topaze (Lt.Cdr. John J.N. Hambly, MBE, RN).
Upon completion of these exercises Lt. Napier took command of HMS H 34 vice Lt. Collett. (14)
18 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Carina (Cdr.(Retd.) T.St.V.F. Tyler, RN) and HMS Tuscarora (Capt.(Retd.) E.C. Stubbs, RN). (14)
19 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN) and HMS Topaze (Lt.Cdr. John J.N. Hambly, MBE, RN). (14)
20 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN) and HMS Carina (Cdr.(Retd.) T.St.V.F. Tyler, RN). (14)
21 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Topaze (Lt.Cdr. John J.N. Hambly, MBE, RN) and HMS Carina (Cdr.(Retd.) T.St.V.F. Tyler, RN). (14)
23 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Tuscarora (Capt.(Retd.) E.C. Stubbs, RN) and HMS Terje 1 (Skr. C. Coultas, DSC, RNR). (14)
24 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Tuscarora (Capt.(Retd.) E.C. Stubbs, RN) and HMS Terje 1 (Skr. C. Coultas, DSC, RNR). (14)
25 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Tuscarora (Capt.(Retd.) E.C. Stubbs, RN) and HMS Terje 1 (Skr. C. Coultas, DSC, RNR). (14)
26 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) and HMS Terje 2 (Ch.Skr. G.R. Gale, RNR). (14)

27 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Tuscarora (Capt.(Retd.) E.C. Stubbs, RN), HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN) and HMS Terje 1 (Skr. C. Coultas, DSC, RNR). (14)

28 Sep 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Topaze (Lt.Cdr. John J.N. Hambly, MBE, RN) and HMS Terje 2 (Ch.Skr. G.R. Gale, RNR). Upon completion of these exercises HMS H 34 proceeded to Rothesay. (14)
2 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) departed Rothesay for Scapa Flow. She is escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (15)
4 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) arrived at Scapa Flow. (15)
6 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with HMS Quorn (Lt. E.A.F. Drought, RN) and HMS Cleveland (Lt.Cdr. W.S. Clouston, RN). (15)

7 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with HMS Nigeria (Capt. J.G.L. Dundas, RN, flying the flag of Rear-Admiral H.M. Burrough, CB, RN) and HMS Cleveland (Lt.Cdr. W.S. Clouston, RN). (15)
8 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with HMS Quorn (Lt. E.A.F. Drought, RN) and HMS Cleveland (Lt.Cdr. W.S. Clouston, RN). (15)

10 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with HMS Nigeria (Capt. J.G.L. Dundas, RN, flying the flag of Rear-Admiral H.M. Burrough, CB, RN) and HMS Quorn (Lt. E.A.F. Drought, RN). (15)
13 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with with local defence craft, HMS Douglas (Cdr.(Retd.) J.G. Crossley, RN). (15)
14 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with with local defence craft, HMS Douglas (Cdr.(Retd.) J.G. Crossley, RN). (15)
15 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with HMS Eskimo (Cdr. St.J.A. Micklethwait, DSO and Bar, RN) and HMS Cleveland (Lt.Cdr. W.S. Clouston, RN). (15)
16 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted special trials off / at Scapa Flow with HMS Dido (Capt. H.W.U. McCall, RN) and HMS Mendip (A/Cdr. G.N. Rolfe, DSC, RN). (15)

17 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with with local defence craft, HMS Douglas (Cdr.(Retd.) J.G. Crossley, RN) and HMS Quorn (Lt. E.A.F. Drought, RN). (15)

18 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with HMS Quorn (Lt. E.A.F. Drought, RN), HMS Cleveland (Lt.Cdr. W.S. Clouston, RN) and HMS Mendip (A/Cdr. G.N. Rolfe, DSC, RN). (15)
19 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off / at Scapa Flow with HMS Quorn (Lt. E.A.F. Drought, RN). (15)
20 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) departed Scapa Flow for Rothesay. She joined convoy EN 12 around dusk for passage through the Minches. (15)
27 Oct 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) arrived at Rothesay. She was escorted in (from 1025 hours) by the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 8 (Lt.Cdr. P.A. de Boer, RNN). (15)
3 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) departed Rothesay for Tobermory. She was escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (16)
4 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) arrived at Tobermory. (16)
5 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Candytuft (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) F.B. Collinson, RD, RNR). (16)
10 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Snapdragon (Lt.Cdr. E.C. Hulton, RN) and FFL Chevreul (Lt. F.P.E. Fourlinnie). (16)
12 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Star of India (Cdr.(Retd.) S.F. Russell, RN) and HMS Jonquil (Lt.Cdr. R.E.H. Partington, RNR). (16)
13 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Star of India (Cdr.(Retd.) S.F. Russell, RN). (16)
14 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Snapdragon (Lt.Cdr. E.C. Hulton, RN) and HMS Jonquil (Lt.Cdr. R.E.H. Partington, RNR). (16)
15 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) proceeded from Tobermory to Oban and back to Tobermory. HMS H 34 proceeded to Oban to fuel. She was escorted by HMS Spindrift (Ch.Skr. A. Gove, RNR). (16)
16 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with FFL Chevreul (Lt. F.P.E. Fourlinnie). (16)
19 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Heather (Cdr.(Retd.) J.G.C. Gibson, RN) and HMS Rumba (T/Lt. N.E. Hendy, RNVR). (16)
21 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Heather (Cdr.(Retd.) J.G.C. Gibson, RN) and HMS Rumba (T/Lt. N.E. Hendy, RNVR). (16)
22 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Star of India (Cdr.(Retd.) S.F. Russell, RN) and HMS Heather (Cdr.(Retd.) J.G.C. Gibson, RN). (16)
25 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Freesia (Lt.Cdr. T.G. Hill, RNR) and HMS Van Oost. (16)
27 Nov 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. S.A. Jones, RNR) and HMS Van Oost. (16)
1 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) departed obermory for Rothesay. She was escorted by HMS Freesia (Lt.Cdr. T.G. Hill, RNR). (17)
2 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (17)
16 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (17)

17 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN). The exercises had to be broken off as due to a malfuntion H 34 flooded her main engines. (17)
18 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) shifted from Campbeltown to Rothesay for repairs. (17)
19 Dec 1940 After repairs, HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN), shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (17)
20 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN). (17)
21 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted A/S exercises of Campbeltown with HMS Terje 1 (Skr. C. Coultas, DSC, RNR). (17)
22 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) shifted from Campbeltown to Rothesay. (17)
24 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (17)
30 Dec 1940 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) departed Rothesay for Rosyth. She made the passage in convoy. (17)
2 Jan 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) arrived at Rosyth. (18)
4 Jan 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) departed Rosyth for Sheerness. She made the passage in convoy FS 79. (18)
7 Jan 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. L.W. Napier, RN) arrived at Sheerness where she was taken in had for refit by the Royal Dockyard. (18)
8 Mar 1941 With her refit completed, HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN), departed Sheerness for Methill. She made the passage in convoy FN 427. (19)
11 Mar 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) arrived at Methill. (19)
14 Mar 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) departed Methill for Tobermory. (19)
16 Mar 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) arrived at Tobermory for A/S exercises. (19)

22 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) arrived at Tobermory. She departed later the same day togther with HMS H 33 (Lt. C.H. Rankin, RN) and HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) for Falmouth. The submarines were escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (20)
25 Mar 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) arrived at Falmouth with a defect to a propeller shaft. (19)
26 Mar 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) departed Falmouth for Plymouth. She was escorted by the Dutch auxiliary M/S trawler HrMs IJmuiden (Lt. H. Zwanenburg, RNN). (19)
27 Mar 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) arrived at Plymouth. (19)
29 Mar 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) departed Plymouth for her 11th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay.
For the daily positions of HMS H 34 during this patrol see the map below. [No positions are known for 29 March to 31 March.]
14 Apr 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) ended her 8th war patrol and HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) ended her 11th war patrol at Falmouth.
At 0630 hours, the submarines made rendez-vous was with their escort towards Falmouth, HMS White Bear (Cdr.(Retd.) C.C. Flemming, RN). (3)

16 Dec 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. W.A. Phillimore, RN) arrived at Rothesay. She departed again later the same day for her 12th war patrol in the Bay of Biscay. Passage south through the Irish Sea was made together with HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) and HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN). They were escorted by HMS Boarhound (Skr. S.G. Jinks, RNR) until 0700/17 when HMS Alecto (Cdr. R.M.G. Gambier, RN) took over the escort until 0500/19 while off Wolf Rock. (32)
19 Dec 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. W.A. Phillimore, RN) arrived at Falmouth. She was not able to go on patrol due to problems with the rudder. (32)
26 Dec 1941 HMS H 34 (Lt. W.A. Phillimore, RN) departed Falmouth for her 13th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay.
1 Jan 1942 HMS H 34 (Lt. W.A. Phillimore, RN) ended her 13th war patrol at Falmouth. (3)

2 Jan 1942 HMS H 32 (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSC, RN), HMS H 33 (Lt. J.H. Bromage, DSC, RN) and HMS H 34 (Lt. W.A. Phillimore, RN) departed Falmouth for Rothesay. They were escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (33)

18 Jan 1942 HMS H 34 (Lt. R. Gatehouse, DSC, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area (Loch Ranza) where she anchored upon completing the exercises. (34)

19 Jan 1942 On board, HMS H 34 (Lt. R. Gatehouse, DSC, RN), it was found out that the no.1 battery tank was taking in water. 30 cells from no.1 battery were damaged. The boat then proceeded to Rothesay where it was found out that there was a leak between no.1 battery tank and no.2 main ballast tank. (34)
21 Jan 1942 HMS H 34 (Lt. R. Gatehouse, DSC, RN) proceeded to Glasgow for repairs to her no.1 battery tank. (34)
28 Jan 1942 With the repairs completed, HMS H 34 (Lt. R. Gatehouse, DSC, RN), returned to Rothesay. (34)
For the daily positions of HMS H 34 during her (abandoned) 14th war patrol see the map below.
29 Jan 1942 HMS H 34 (Lt. R. Gatehouse, DSC, RN) departed Rothesay for her 14th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay.
Passage south through the Irish Sea was made together with HMS H 50 (Lt. H.B. Turner, RN). During their passage south, the submarines were escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (34)
31 Jan 1942 HMS H 34 (Lt. R. Gatehouse, DSC, RN) had to proceed to Falmouth with one defective engine air compressor. She had to abandon her 14th war patrol. (34)

2 Sep 1942 HMS H 34 (Lt. J.P.H. Oakley, DSC, RN) conducted exercises off Scapa Flow. These exercises included a practice attack on the battleship HMS Anson (Capt. H.R.G. Kinahan, CBE, RN, flying the flag of Vice-Admiral Sir B. Fraser, CB, KBE, RN) and her screen of six destroyers; HMS Onslow (Capt H.T. Armstrong, DSC and Bar, RN), HMS Onslaught (Cdr. W.H. Selby, RN), HMS Opportune (Cdr. M.L. Power, OBE, RN), HMS Eskimo (Cdr. E.G. Le Geyt, RN), HMS Tartar (Cdr. St.J.R.J. Tyrwhitt, DSC, RN) and HMS Echo (Lt.Cdr. N. Lanyon, RN).

On completion of the exercises the destroyers HMS Ashanti (Cdr. R.G. Onslow, DSO, RN) and HMS Somali (Lt.Cdr. C.D. Maud, DSC and Bar, RN) also joined after which the surface ships all set course for Hvalfjord, Iceland. (44)
